essence: Protects and enhances biodiversity by assessing habitats, guiding land‑use decisions, and supporting nature‑recovery initiatives.

identity.title: Biodiversity Officer
identity.summary: Assesses habitats and species, advises on biodiversity protection, supports planning decisions, and delivers nature‑recovery and conservation projects.

salary.notes: Senior biodiversity specialists or strategic leads can exceed £60k-£75k+.

qualifications: Training in ecology, conservation, or environmental science
Knowledge of biodiversity legislation and planning policy
Ability to conduct habitat and species assessments
Understanding of nature‑recovery strategies and ecosystem management

lifestyle.description: Biodiversity officers work for councils, conservation bodies, environmental agencies, and consultancies. They assess habitats, guide planning decisions, support conservation projects, and help deliver nature‑recovery strategies. The role suits individuals who enjoy ecology, policy, fieldwork, and long‑term environmental impact.

habits: Advises on planning applications and land‑use decisions
Engages with landowners, communities, and stakeholders
Monitors species, habitats, and environmental change
